prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: TaraHome technical blog cover, landscape
Primary request: Create a topic-specific editorial photograph for an article asking whether Matter smart-home devices keep working after their manufacturer closes or ends cloud support.
Scene/backdrop: a warm, lived-in residential living room with an oak console, plaster wall, linen and woven textures, and soft daylight.
Subject: a compact unbranded local smart-home hub and home router remain powered on beside a physical smart plug that is visibly powering a warm table lamp; a discreet door sensor is installed nearby. A closed, slightly faded unbranded smart-home product box and a face-down phone sit off to the side, suggesting the old vendor app and company are no longer central while the local home still works. All hardware must look plausible, grounded, and generic rather than copied from a real brand.
Style/medium: clean high-end residential editorial photography, realistic materials and proportions, calm and practical rather than futuristic.
Composition/framing: wide 3:2 or 16:10 landscape; keep the active local hub, plug, and glowing lamp clearly legible in both the full cover and a centered 16:9 article-card crop; no empty title-poster area.
Lighting/mood: natural late-afternoon light plus the warm working lamp, reassuring but honest, realistic soft shadows.
Color palette: warm cream, oak, linen, charcoal hardware, and restrained dark-green accents.
Materials/textures: real wood grain, matte device plastic, aged plain cardboard, woven fabric, subtle plaster texture.
Constraints: no embedded words, labels, numbers, headlines, logos, brand marks, watermark, or readable screens; no invented branded hardware; no decorative routing, network, path, glow, or connection-line overlays; no diagrams; no translucent panels; no inset frames; no floating UI cards; no progress bars; no fake charts or graphs; no poster treatment; no floating devices.
Avoid: generic AI smart-home diagrams, corporate office scenes, abandoned or apocalyptic imagery, sci-fi server rooms, neon, blue-purple gradients, repeated beige device diagrams, cable clutter, duplicated hardware, surreal devices, warning symbols, gravestones, clouds with strike-through icons.
